subject: English
chapter: The Case of the Fifth Word
source_pdf: The Case of the Fifth Word.pdf

key_points: Encyclopedia Brown is a young boy known for solving mysteries using his intelligence
His real name is Leroy Brown and his father is the Chief of Police
Tim Nolan was suspected of being involved in a jewellery robbery
Before dying, Nolan left a mysterious coded message with four strange words
Encyclopedia Brown solved the mystery by identifying the hidden fifth word
examples: Chief Brown often discussed difficult cases with Encyclopedia at the dinner table
The four coded words were Nom, Utes, Sweden, and Hurts
These words represented the days Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday
The missing fifth word represented Friday
The jewellery was hidden under a young fir tree in Nolan’s nursery
formulas_or_processes: Problem → Mystery of the hidden jewellery
Clue → Four strange coded words written on a calendar sheet
Analysis → Encyclopedia recognizes the pattern of days of the week
Solution → The missing fifth word points to the fir tree
Resolution → The hidden jewellery is discovered
exam_tips: Remember that Encyclopedia Brown solves mysteries using logic and observation
Understand how the four coded words represented days of the week
Focus on the importance of the missing fifth word in solving the case
Know the lesson: careful thinking and observation can solve difficult problems
